zh.Bristol, Gloucestershire, England: 英國格盧斯特斯爾布里斯托
zh.lifeSummary: 當Julian le Prouz於1316年之前出生在英國戴文吉德利時，她的父親William Prouz II15歲，母親Elena de Ponte15歲。她於1340年與Richard de Wotton在英國戴文結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於大約1406年在英國戴文哈福德。

zh-hans.Bristol, Gloucestershire, England: 英国格盧斯特斯爾布里斯托
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Julian le Prouz于1316年之前出生在英国戴文吉德利时，她的父亲William Prouz II15岁，母亲Elena de Ponte15岁。她于1340年与Richard de Wotton在英国戴文结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于大约1406年在英国戴文哈福德。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Prouz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: nickname from Broz, an old Bavarian word meaning ‘toad’ and, in a figurative sense, ‘showy’.
German: habitational name from any of various places in Brandenburg, Lower Saxony, and Luxembourg called Protz(en).
Germanized form of Sorbian Proca or Próca: nickname from Upper Sorbian proca, Lower Sorbian próca ‘trouble, (hard) work’.

nameMeanings.name: Julian
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the common Late Latin name Julianus, a derivative of Julius . In classical times Julianus was a name borne not only by various minor early saints, but also by the Roman emperor Julian ‘the Apostate’, who attempted to return the Roman Empire from institutionalized Christianity to paganism. For many centuries the English name Julian was borne by women as well as men, for example by the Blessed Julian of Norwich ( c.1342– after 1413 ). The differentiation in form between Julian and Gillian did not develop until the 16th century. Julian is still occasionally used as a girl's name. Notable bearers include the British classical guitarist Julian Bream ( b. 1933 ) and the British jazz pianist and bandleader Julian Joseph ( b. 1966 ).

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Julian le Prouz was born before 1316, in Gidleigh, Devon, England, her father, William Prouz II, was 15 and her mother, Elena de Ponte, was 15. She married Richard de Wotton in 1340, in Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died about 1406, in Chagford, Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Julian le Prouz est née avant 1316, à Gidleigh, Devon, Angleterre, son père, William Prouz II, avait 15 ans et sa mère, Elena de Ponte, avait 15 ans. Elle a épousé Richard de Wotton en 1340, à Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée environ 1406, à Chagford, Devon, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Julian le Prouz vor 1316 in Gidleigh, Devon, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Prouz II 15 und ihre Mutter Elena de Ponte 15. Sie hat 1340 in Devon, England Richard de Wotton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb um 1406 in Chagford, Devon, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Julian le Prouz è nata il prima del 1316 nel Gidleigh, Devon, England, sua padre, William Prouz II, aveva 15 anni e sua madre, Elena de Ponte, aveva 15 anni. Lei sposato Richard de Wotton nel 1340, nel Devon, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì circa 1406, nel Chagford, Devon, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Julian le Prouzが1316年以前にイギリスデボンギドレイグで生まれた時，父William Prouz IIは15歳，母Elena de Ponteは15歳であった。1340年にイギリスデボンでRichard de Wottonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は約1406年にイギリスデボンチャグフォードで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Julian le Prouz(이)가 1316년 이전 영국 데폰 지들리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Prouz II(은)는 15세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elena de Ponte(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 1340년 영국 데폰에서 Richard de Wotton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1406년 영국 데폰 차그퍼드에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Julian le Prouz nasceu antes 1316, em Gidleigh, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Prouz II, tinha 15 anos e sua mãe, Elena de Ponte, tinha 15 anos. Ela casou-se com Richard de Wotton em 1340, em Devon, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1406, em Chagford, Devon, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Julian le Prouz родилась перед 1316 г. в местности гидлиг, девон, Англия, её отцу по имени William Prouz II было 15 лет, а матери, которую звали Elena de Ponte, 15. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard de Wotton в 1340 г. в местности девон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла около 1406 г. в местности чагфорд, девон, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Julian le Prouz nació antes de 1316, en Gidleigh, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, William Prouz II, tenía 15 años y su mamá, Elena de Ponte, tenía 15 años. Se casó con Richard de Wotton en 1340, en Devon, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió aproximadamente 1406, en Chagford, Devon, Inglaterra.
